text.title: terraform Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for terraform. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Split configuration into main.tf, variables.tf, outputs.tf, versions.tf, providers.tf, locals.tf, and data.tf.
rule.rationale: Each file has a single responsibility; reviewers and tools know where to look for each element type.

rule.statement: Pin required_version with a minimum bound (e.g., >= 1.6.0) and pin provider versions with ~> constraints. Commit .terraform.lock.hcl.
rule.rationale: Unpinned versions cause silent upgrades that break apply; lock file ensures deterministic runs across machines and CI.

rule.statement: Every variable must have a description. Mark sensitive values with sensitive = true. Add validation blocks for constrained inputs.
rule.rationale: Sensitive flag redacts values from plan output and state display. Validation provides clear error messages earlier than resource creation.

rule.statement: Use locals to compute derived values and naming prefixes. Never repeat the same expression in multiple resources.
rule.rationale: DRY locals prevent inconsistent naming and make bulk renames a one-line change.

rule.statement: Mark outputs that contain secrets as sensitive = true. Provide descriptions for all outputs.
rule.rationale: Sensitive outputs are redacted in CI logs and terraform output commands.

rule.statement: Prefer for_each over count for resources that may be updated mid-lifecycle to avoid index-based drift.
rule.rationale: count uses numeric indexes; removing item[1] of 3 forces destroy/recreate of item[2]. for_each uses stable keys.

rule.statement: Use merge(local.common_tags, { Name = "..." }) to combine tag maps consistently across all resources.
rule.rationale: Avoids duplicating the common_tags map in every resource block.

rule.statement: Use remote backend with encryption enabled. For AWS: S3 bucket with SSE-KMS, versioning, public access blocked, and DynamoDB table for locking.
rule.rationale: Local state cannot be shared across team members or CI/CD. S3 versioning enables state recovery. DynamoDB locking prevents concurrent apply corruption.

rule.statement: Isolate state per environment (envs/dev/, envs/prod/) and per component (network/, compute/). Never share a single state file across environments.
rule.rationale: Shared state means a broken dev apply can corrupt prod state. Isolation contains blast radius.

rule.statement: Use moved blocks in HCL for resource renames, not terraform state mv. Verify with plan showing no changes after the move.
rule.rationale: moved blocks are version-controlled and self-documenting. terraform state mv is a one-off command that leaves no trace in code.

rule.statement: Each module has single responsibility. Required files: main.tf, variables.tf, outputs.tf, versions.tf. Never configure providers inside modules.
rule.rationale: Provider configuration in modules prevents caller from controlling the provider version/region, making modules inflexible.

rule.statement: All module inputs must have descriptions. Add validation rules for constrained inputs. Provide sensible defaults for optional variables.
rule.rationale: Without descriptions, callers cannot understand what a variable does. Validation surfaces errors at plan time, not at the resource API level.

rule.statement: Never store secrets in Terraform code, tfvars files, or state. Reference secrets via data sources (AWS Secrets Manager, Vault).
rule.rationale: State files are stored in S3 and may be logged in CI. Secrets in code are committed to version control. External secret managers provide rotation and audit trails.

rule.statement: Use IAM roles, not users. Apply least-privilege: no wildcard actions except read-only describe calls. Add resource-level conditions where available.
rule.rationale: Roles use temporary credentials. Wildcard actions grant more permissions than required; resource conditions restrict to tagged/named resources.

rule.statement: In CI: run terraform fmt -check, terraform validate, and security scanning (tfsec/checkov) before plan. Save plan as artifact; apply only from protected branch using the saved plan.
rule.rationale: fmt -check catches formatting drift early. Saved plan ensures apply uses exactly the reviewed plan, preventing race conditions if code changes between plan and apply.
